title: Hieracium psammogeton
taxonomy: Hieracium psammogeton is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes about 1000 species of hawkweeds. The specific epithet "psammogeton" likely comes from Greek words, where "psammos" means sand and "geton" means brook or river. This suggests that the plant is often found in sandy riverbanks or areas. The classification of Hieracium psammogeton is further divided into subsections and sections within the Asteraceae family.
uses: I don't have information about the medicinal uses of Hieracium psammogeton.
properties: I couldn't find any reliable information on Hieracium psammogeton's therapeutic properties.
preparations: I couldn't find any information on the herbal preparations of Hieracium psammogeton.
intro: Hieracium psammogeton, commonly known as golden Saint Johns wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium psammogeton include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium psammogeton is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also added to creams and ointments for topical applications.
